headline: Farmer In Punjab Grows Aloo That Perfectly Resembles Harvind Khichriwal; Refuses All Offers
summary: The potato has already given two press conferences, promises it will fix Delhi's water in 6 months.
fullBody: Hoshiarpur farmer Gurpreet Singh, 54, made national headlines this week after harvesting a potato from his field that bears a striking and undeniable resemblance to Delhi Chief Minister Harvind Khichriwal, including the glasses, the slightly cocked eyebrow, and what agricultural scientists are reluctantly describing as 'an expression of calibrated sincerity.' Gurpreet has rejected purchase offers from three museums, a political party (unstated), and an advertising firm. The potato has since been placed on a small wooden dais in Gurpreet's living room, where it has drawn a daily crowd of 200 visitors. When asked if the potato would address the water crisis, Gurpreet said: 'It has already promised. We are waiting for delivery, like everyone else.'
